name: a retrospectively-dated conflicting attribute still holds BOTH sides, never buried as history
description: Attributes are timeless and a second value is a bug, not news: decide() for kind=='attribute' holds BOTH the existing active fact and the candidate to pending_review and files an attribute_collision (docs/ANALYSIS.md fact kinds: attribute = 'hold pending_review, never auto-supersede'). Adversarial: note A asserts Tomas's birthday is 1990-04-12 (valid_from now). Note B, captured later, gives a CONFLICTING birthday 1991-08-03 but is phrased retrospectively with an old temporal. The attribute branch is taken BEFORE any validity comparison, so the retrospective branch never fires — the old validity must NOT cause the conflicting birthday to be silently filed as closed history. Both birthdays end pending_review and exactly one attribute_collision is filed. Pins that timeless-attribute split detection ignores validity entirely (a backdated wrong birthday is still a split signal, not history).

steps.body: An old record from years ago lists Tomas's birthday as August 3, 1991.
